title: Fail closed on malformed OAuth destination queries
verify: Malformed OAuth query segments, invalid percent escapes, encoded controls, empty names, empty segments, and double-encoded callback/return keys fail closed.
Legitimate Google/VK OAuth query parameters on the exact approved paths still pass.
All prior TASK-030 path, origin, concurrency, storage, cart, and no-token checks remain green.

verify.reason: Operator instructed scheduler to continue remediation without repeated per-fix approval prompts.
Independent T3 verification found malformed percent after decode, invalid decoded names, and missing query size/count caps.
T3 functional and semantic verification passed after bounded retry 1/2.

constraints: Parse and validate the raw query before URL normalization loses malformed structure.
Do not reset TASK-030 without operator approval.
invariants: Malformed or ambiguous query syntax fails closed.
Dangerous keys fail closed after bounded decoding.
purpose: Close the remaining malformed-query bypass in the OAuth start destination validator.
success_outcome: Only structurally valid legitimate OAuth queries pass on exact approved provider paths.
anti_goals: Do not broaden provider origins or paths.
Do not change backend provider exchange.
Do not weaken prior TASK-030 fixes.

stop_conditions: A bounded raw-query parser cannot distinguish legitimate and malformed input.
The fix requires broadening approved destinations.
